translations.literal: The Man and Yi were shaken and afraid; they immediately together beheaded their great chief and surrendered; thereupon he entered the bandit camp, dispersed their masses, sent them back to native commanderies, for them set chief officials and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The tribes panicked, slew their own chieftain, and submitted. Jun entered their camps, broke up the hosts, sent them home to their counties with new magistrates, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 均未至，先自劾矯製之罪。

translations.literal: Before Jun arrived, he first impeached himself for the crime of falsely assuming mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Before he reached the capital he memorialized his own forgery of imperial orders.

sentences.zh: 光武嘉其功，迎賜以金帛，令過家上塚。

translations.literal: Guangwu praised his merit, welcomed and granted him with gold and silks, ordered him to pass home and ascend tombs.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu approved the victory, rewarded him with gold and silk, and told him to visit his family graves on the way home.

sentences.zh: 其後每有四方異議，數訪問焉。

translations.literal: Afterward whenever there were four-directions unusual debates, he several times consulted him.
translations.idiomatic: After that the emperor often sought his counsel whenever frontier policy was disputed.

sentences.zh: 遷上蔡令。

translations.literal: He was transferred to be magistrate of Shangcai.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to magistrate of Shangcai.

sentences.zh: 時府下記，禁人喪葬不得侈長。

translations.literal: At that time the prefecture issued a note forbidding people's funerals and burials from being extravagant and lengthy.
translations.idiomatic: The prefecture ordered limits on funeral extravagance and duration.

sentences.zh: 均曰：「夫送終逾制，失之輕者。

translations.literal: Jun said: "As for sending off the end exceeding regulation—that is a fault of the light kind.
translations.idiomatic: Jun replied, "Burials that break sumptuary rules are a minor fault.

sentences.zh: 今有不義之民，尚未循化，而遽罰過禮，非政之先。

translations.literal: Now there are unrighteous people who have not yet followed transformation, yet hastily punishing transgression of ritual is not government putting first.
translations.idiomatic: Unruly people have not yet accepted instruction—punishing funeral excess before anything else is the wrong priority for government.

sentences.zh: 」竟不肯施行。

translations.literal: " In the end he refused to implement it.
translations.idiomatic: He refused to enforce the order.

sentences.zh: 永平元年，遷東海相，在郡五年，坐法免官，客授潁川。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongping he was transferred to chancellor of the East Sea; he was in the commandery five years, sat guilty by law and was dismissed from office, and as guest taught in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 1 he became chancellor of the East Sea. Five years later a legal charge removed him from office, and he taught privately in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 而東海吏民思均恩化，為之作歌，詣闕乞還者數千人。

translations.literal: But East Sea clerks and people thought on Jun's gracious transformation, made songs for him; those who went to the gate begging his return were several thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Yet East Sea officials and commoners missed his rule, composed songs in his honor, and thousands petitioned at the palace gate for his recall.

sentences.zh: 顯宗以其能，七年，徵拜尚書令。

translations.literal: Emperor Ming on account of his ability, in the seventh year, summoned and appointed him director of the secretariat.
translations.idiomatic: Impressed by his record, Emperor Ming named him director of the secretariat in the seventh year of his reign.

sentences.zh: 每有駁議，多合上旨。

translations.literal: Whenever there was refutation debate, mostly matched the sovereign's intent.
translations.idiomatic: His dissenting opinions usually matched the emperor's wishes.

sentences.zh: 均嘗刪剪疑事，帝以為有奸，大怒，收郎縛格之。

translations.literal: Jun once deleted and trimmed doubtful affairs; the emperor thought there was treachery and was greatly angry; he seized the gentleman and bound him to the frame.
translations.idiomatic: Once Jun cut passages from a doubtful case file; the emperor suspected foul play, flew into a rage, and had the clerk seized for the cane.

sentences.zh: 諸尚書惶恐，皆叩頭謝罪。

translations.literal: The various secretaries were frightened; all knocked heads apologizing for guilt.
translations.idiomatic: The secretaries panicked and begged forgiveness on their knees.

sentences.zh: 均顧厲色曰：「蓋忠臣執義，無有二心。

translations.literal: Jun looked back with stern color and said: "A loyal minister holds to righteousness; there is no second heart.
translations.idiomatic: Jun turned with a fierce look and said, "A loyal minister holds one duty—no second thoughts.

sentences.zh: 若畏威失正，均雖死，不易誌。

translations.literal: If in fearing awe one loses rectitude, although Jun dies, he does not change intent.
translations.idiomatic: If I feared your wrath and betrayed the right, I could die branded a coward—I will not swerve."

sentences.zh: 」小黃門在傍，入具以聞。

translations.literal: " A minor yellow gate was at the side; he entered and fully reported it on hearing.
translations.idiomatic: A young eunuch attendant overheard and carried the whole exchange to the emperor.

sentences.zh: 帝善其不撓，即令貰郎，遷均司隸校尉。

translations.literal: The emperor approved his not yielding; immediately ordered pardon the gentleman; promoted Jun to Metropolitan Superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired his backbone, spared the clerk, and promoted Song Jun to metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 數月，出為河內太守，政化大行。

translations.literal: After several months he went out as governor of Henei; government transformation greatly proceeded.
translations.idiomatic: Months later he became governor of Henei, where his reforms flourished.

sentences.zh: 均嘗寢病，百姓耆老為禱請，旦夕問起居，其為民愛若此。

translations.literal: Jun once lay ill; common people and elders prayed for him; morning and evening they asked after rising and rest—he was loved by the people like this.
translations.idiomatic: When Song Jun fell ill, elders and villagers prayed for him and asked after him day and night—such was their devotion.

sentences.zh: 以疾上書乞免，詔除子條為太子舍人。

translations.literal: On account of illness he submitted a memorial begging dismissal; an edict appointed his son Tiao as attendant to the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: He asked leave on grounds of health; the court appointed his son Song Tiao attendant to the crown prince.

sentences.zh: 均自扶輿詣闕謝恩，帝使中黃門慰問，因留養疾。

translations.literal: Jun himself supported carriage and went to the gate thanking grace; the emperor sent a central yellow gate to console and inquire, and thereby retained him to nurse illness.
translations.idiomatic: Song Jun had himself carried to the palace to give thanks; the emperor sent a eunuch to console him and kept him at court to convalesce.

sentences.zh: 司徒缺，帝以均才任宰相，召入視其疾，令兩騶扶之。

translations.literal: The minister of education was vacant; the emperor considered Jun's talent fit for minister and summoned him in to view his illness, ordered two outriders to support him.
translations.idiomatic: When the minister of education post stood empty, the emperor judged Song Jun fit for high office and summoned him to court despite his illness, ordering attendants to support him on both sides.

sentences.zh: 均拜謝曰：「天罰有罪，所苦浸篤，不復奉望帷幄！

translations.literal: Jun bowed and thanked, saying: "Heaven punishes the guilty; what I suffer gradually worsens; I no longer can serve looking on the tent curtain!
translations.idiomatic: Song Jun bowed and said, "Heaven punishes sin; my pain grows worse—I can no longer serve in the imperial council."

sentences.zh: 」因流涕而辭。

translations.literal: " Thereupon weeping he declined.
translations.idiomatic: Tears streaming, he declined.

sentences.zh: 帝甚傷之，召條扶侍均出，賜錢三十萬。

translations.literal: The emperor greatly grieved for him; summoned Tiao to support and attend Jun out; granted three hundred thousand cash.
translations.idiomatic: Deeply moved, the emperor called Song Tiao to escort his father home and gave three hundred thousand cash.

sentences.zh: 意字伯志。

translations.literal: Yi, styled Bozhi.
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi was styled Bozhi.

sentences.zh: 父京，以《大夏侯尚書》教授，至遼東太守。

translations.literal: Father Jing, by the Large Xia Hou Documents instructed, reached Liaodong governor.
translations.idiomatic: His father Jing taught the Large Xia Hou recension of the Documents and rose to governor of Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 意少傳父業，顯宗時舉孝廉，以召對合旨，擢拜阿陽侯相。

translations.literal: Yi in youth transmitted father's enterprise; in Emperor Ming's time was recommended filial and incorrupt; because summoned audience matched intent, was promoted and appointed chancellor of the marquisate of Ayang.
translations.idiomatic: Yi studied under his father. Under Emperor Ming he was recommended filial and incorrupt; an audience pleased the throne, and he was named chancellor of the Ayang marquisate.

sentences.zh: 建初中，徵為尚書。

translations.literal: In the Jianchu period he was summoned as secretary.
translations.idiomatic: During Jianchu he was summoned to the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 肅宗性寬仁，而親親之恩篤，故叔父濟南、中山二王每數入朝，特加恩寵，及諸昆弟並留京師，不遣就國。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang's nature was generous and benevolent, and the grace of loving kin was thick; therefore uncles Jinan and Zhongshan two kings several times entered court, specially increased grace and favor, and all brothers together stayed in the capital, not sent to their states.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang was kindly and devoted to his kin: the kings of Jinan and Zhongshan—his junior uncles—visited court often with extraordinary favor, and his brothers were kept in the capital rather than sent to their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 意以為人臣有節，不宜逾禮過恩，乃上疏諫曰：「陛下至孝烝烝，恩受隆深，以濟南王康、中山王焉先帝昆弟，特蒙禮寵，聖情戀戀，不忍遠離，比年朝見，久留京師，崇以叔父之尊，同之家人之禮，車入殿門，即席不拜，分甘損膳，賞賜優渥。

translations.literal: Yi thought human ministers had restraint; it was not fitting to exceed ritual and pass grace; then submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying: "Your Majesty utmost filial piety steaming; grace received lofty and deep; because Jinan King Kang and Zhongshan King Yan are former emperor's brothers, specially receive ritual favor; sagely feeling lingering, cannot bear far separation; in recent years court audience, long stayed capital; honor them with uncle's dignity, treat same as family ritual; cart enters hall gate, at mat do not bow; share sweet and reduce meals; gifts and grants generous.
translations.idiomatic: Yi believed ministers must uphold propriety and that favor must not breach ritual. He memorialized: "Your filial devotion runs deep. The kings Kang of Jinan and Yan of Zhongshan—your father's brothers—enjoy extraordinary favor; your affection keeps them year after year in the capital. You honor them as uncles yet treat them like kin at home: their carriages enter the hall doors; they need not bow from their mats; you share your table and heap gifts on them.

sentences.zh: 昔周公懷聖人之德，有致太平之功，然後王曰叔父，加以錫幣。

translations.literal: Formerly the Duke of Zhou embraced the sage's virtue, had the merit of bringing great peace; afterward the king called him uncle and added metal and silks.
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Zhou earned the title 'uncle' and gifts of metal only after his virtue had brought peace to the realm.

sentences.zh: 今康、焉幸以支庶享食大國，陛下即位，蠲除前過，還所削黜，衍食他縣，男女少長，並受爵邑，恩寵逾製，禮敬過度。

translations.literal: Now Kang and Yan luckily by cadet branch enjoy eating great states; Your Majesty ascended the throne, remitted former faults, restored what was cut and demoted, extended food to other counties; men and women young and old together received noble ranks and fiefs; grace and favor exceed regulation; ritual respect exceeds measure.
translations.idiomatic: Kang and Yan are cadet princes feasting on great domains. Since Your Majesty took the throne you have erased old offenses, restored confiscated income, added counties to their allotments, and ennobled every child—grace and ceremony alike overshoot the statutes.

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，諸父昆弟無所不臣，所以尊尊卑卑，強幹弱枝者也。

translations.literal: The Spring and Autumn's meaning: uncles and brothers are nowhere not subjects—this is how to honor the honored and humble the humble, strengthen the trunk and weaken the branches.
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn demands that even uncles serve the throne—honoring rank, strengthening the trunk, weakening the branches.

sentences.zh: 陛下德業隆盛，當為萬世典法，不宜以私恩損上下之序，失君臣之正。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's virtue and enterprise are lofty and flourishing; you ought to be ten thousand generations' norm and law; it is not fitting by private grace to harm upper and lower order, lose ruler and minister's rectitude.
translations.idiomatic: Your virtue should set the pattern for ages; private affection must not upset the order of high and low or corrupt the right relation of sovereign and subject.

sentences.zh: 又西平王羨等六王，皆妻子成家，官屬備具，當早就蕃國，為子孫基阯。

translations.literal: Moreover Western Peace King Xian and other six kings—all have wives and children completing households, officials and retinue fully prepared; ought early to go to their feudatory states, be foundation for descendants.
translations.idiomatic: The six kings including Xian of Xiping have wives, heirs, and full households—they should return promptly to their fiefs and lay foundations for their posterity.

sentences.zh: 而室第相望，久磐京邑，婚姻之盛，過於本朝，仆馬之眾，充塞城郭，驕奢僭擬，寵祿隆過。

translations.literal: Yet mansions face each other, long anchored in the capital; marriage alliances' splendor exceeds this court; servants and horses' multitude fills city walls; arrogance and luxury transgressively imitate; favor and salary lofty beyond measure.
translations.idiomatic: Instead their mansions crowd the capital; intermarriage outshines even the imperial clan; grooms and carriages choke the roads; they swagger like sovereigns and draw salaries beyond reason.

sentences.zh: 今諸國之封，並皆豪腴，風氣平調，道路夷近，朝聘有期，行來不難。

translations.literal: Now the various states' enfeoffments are all rich and fertile; wind and qi are level and mild; roads are smooth and near; court visits have scheduled periods; going and coming is not difficult.
translations.idiomatic: Their fiefs are rich, the climate mild, the roads easy; scheduled audiences make travel undemanding.

sentences.zh: 宜割情不忍，以義斷恩，發遣康、焉各歸蕃國，令羨等速就便時，以塞眾望。

translations.literal: It is fitting to cut emotion one cannot bear, by righteousness sever grace, dispatch Kang and Yan each to return to feudatory states, order Xian and others quickly to take convenient season, to stop the multitude's hope.
translations.idiomatic: Master your affection and sever favor with duty: send Kang and Yan home to their kingdoms and order Xian and his peers to depart at once—this is what the empire expects.

sentences.zh: 」帝納之。

translations.literal: " The emperor accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor accepted the advice.

sentences.zh: 章和二年，鮮卑擊破北匈奴，而南單于乘此請兵北伐，因欲還歸舊庭。

translations.literal: In the second year of Zhanghe, Xianbei struck and broke Northern Xiongnu, and the Southern Chanyu riding this requested troops for northern campaign, thereby wishing to return to old court.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhanghe 2 the Xianbei shattered the Northern Xiongnu. The Southern Chanyu seized the moment to ask for an expedition so he could move back to his old steppe court.

sentences.zh: 時竇太后臨朝，議欲從之。

translations.literal: At that time Empress Dowager Dou faced court; deliberation wished to follow him.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dou held court; the debate leaned toward agreement.

sentences.zh: 意上疏曰：

translations.literal: Yi submitted a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Song Yi addressed the throne:

sentences.zh: 夫戎狄之隔遠中國，幽處北極，界以沙漠，簡賤禮義，無有上下，強者為雄，弱即屈服。

translations.literal: The Rong and Di are separated far from the central states, lurk in the north extreme, bounded by desert sands; they treat ritual and righteousness as simple and base; there is no upper and lower; the strong become heroes; the weak then yield.
translations.idiomatic: The northern tribes live beyond the deserts, scorning our rituals—might alone rules them, and the weak submit.

sentences.zh: 自漢興以來，征伐數矣，其所克獲，曾不補害。

translations.literal: Since Han arose, campaigns have been many; what they conquered and captured has never repaid the harm.
translations.idiomatic: Since the founding of Han, campaigns have multiplied, yet victories rarely repay their cost.

sentences.zh: 光武皇帝躬服金革之難，深昭天地之明，故因其來降，羈縻畜養，邊人得生，勞役休息，於茲四十餘年矣。

translations.literal: Emperor Guangwu personally endured metal and leather's hardship, deeply displayed heaven and earth's clarity; therefore because they came to surrender, haltered and bridled, nourished and raised, border people obtained life, labor service rested—here forty-some years.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu bore the armor himself and read heaven's mind: he settled the surrendering tribes under loose rein so the frontier could live and corvée ease—forty years of quiet.

sentences.zh: 今鮮卑奉順，斬獲萬數，中國坐享大功，而百姓不知其勞，漢興功烈。

translations.literal: Now Xianbei obeys and follows; beheadings and captures count in myriads; the central states sit enjoying great merit, yet the hundred surnames do not know their toil—Han rising merit and brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Xianbei obey and have taken tens of thousands of heads; China reaps the glory without lifting a spear—the greatest feat since the restoration.

sentences.zh: 於斯為盛。

translations.literal: At this it is most splendid.
translations.idiomatic: Nothing since Han's rise matches it.

sentences.zh: 所以然者，夷虜相攻，無損漢兵者也。

translations.literal: The reason it is thus is that barbarians attack each other—there is no harm to Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: Barbarians are bleeding each other while our troops stay home.

sentences.zh: 臣察鮮卑侵伐匈奴，正是利其抄掠，及歸功聖朝，實由貪得重賞。

translations.literal: Your servant examines Xianbei invading and attacking Xiongnu—it is exactly profiting from their plunder; as for returning merit to the sagely court, in truth it comes from greedy obtaining heavy rewards.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbei raid the Xiongnu for plunder; they credit the throne only because they hunger for rich rewards.

sentences.zh: 今若聽南虜還都北庭，則不得不禁製鮮卑。

translations.literal: Now if we listen to the southern barbarians return capital to northern court, then we cannot but forbid and restrain Xianbei.
translations.idiomatic: If we let the Southern Chanyu resettle the northern steppe, we must choke off the Xianbei raids that fuel him—and that they will not endure.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑外失暴掠之願，內無功勞之賞，豺狼貪婪，必為邊患。

translations.literal: Xianbei outside loses violent plunder's desire; inside has no merit reward's gift—wolf-like greed; inevitably will become border disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Deprived of plunder and imperial bounty alike, those wolves will turn on our frontiers.

sentences.zh: 今北虜西遁，請求和親，宜因其歸附，以為外扞，巍巍之業，無以過此。

translations.literal: Now the northern barbarians flee west; they request peace and kinship; it is fitting to ride their attachment and submission, make them outer shield—towering enterprise—nothing surpasses this.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Xiongnu are fleeing west and sue for peace—take them in as an outer shield; no policy could be grander.

sentences.zh: 若引兵費賦，以順南虜，則坐失上略，去安即危矣。

translations.literal: If one leads troops and exhausts revenue to comply with the southern barbarians, then one sits and loses superior strategy; leaving security one immediately meets peril.
translations.idiomatic: To march armies and drain the treasury merely to gratify the Southern Chanyu would abandon the best strategy and trade safety for peril.

sentences.zh: 誠不可許。

translations.literal: Truly cannot be permitted.
translations.idiomatic: The request must be refused.

sentences.zh: 會南單于竟不北徙。

translations.literal: It happened that the Southern Chanyu in the end did not move north.
translations.idiomatic: In the end the Southern Chanyu did not migrate north.

sentences.zh: 遷司隸校尉。

translations.literal: He was transferred to Metropolitan Superintendent.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 永元初，大將軍竇憲兄弟貴盛，步兵校尉鄧疊、河南尹王調、故蜀郡太守廉範等群黨，出入憲門，負勢放縱。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Yongyuan, Grand General Dou Xian's brothers were noble and flourishing; Colonel of Foot Deng Die, Henan Governor Wang Diao, former Shu governor Lian Fan and other faction associates came and went at Xian's gate, relying on power and unrestrained.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Yongyuan the Dou brothers ruled the court. Deng Die, Wang Diao of Henan, former Shu governor Lian Fan, and their clique haunted Dou Xian's gate, swaggering on his influence.

sentences.zh: 意隨違舉奏，無所回避，由是與竇氏有隙。

translations.literal: Yi followed disobedience and raised memorials, nothing avoided or dodged; thereby he had a rift with the Dou clan.
translations.idiomatic: Yi impeached wrongdoing wherever he found it and spared no one—so he fell out with the Dou clan.

sentences.zh: 二年，病卒。

translations.literal: In the second year he died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: He died of illness in the second year.

sentences.zh: 孫俱，靈帝時為司空。

translations.literal: Sun Ju, in Emperor Ling's time was minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: His descendant Sun Ju served as minister of works under Emperor Ling.

sentences.zh: 寒朗字伯奇，魯國薛人也。

translations.literal: Han Lang, styled Boqi, was a man of Xue in the Lu kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: Han Lang, courtesy name Boqi, came from Xue in the kingdom of Lu.

sentences.zh: 生三日，遭天下亂，棄之荊刺；

translations.literal: Three days after birth, he encountered the realm in chaos; they abandoned him in thorns and brambles;
translations.idiomatic: He was born into rebellion; on the third day his family abandoned him in a bramble patch;

sentences.zh: 數日兵解，母往視，猶尚氣息，遂收養之。

translations.literal: After several days the soldiers dispersed; the mother went to look; he still had breath; thereupon she collected and raised him.
translations.idiomatic: when the fighting moved on his mother found him still breathing and took him home.

sentences.zh: 及長，好經學，博通書傳，以《尚書》教授。

translations.literal: When grown, he loved classical learning, broadly comprehended documents and commentaries, and by the Documents instructed.
translations.idiomatic: As a man he devoted himself to the classics, mastered the literature of the canon, and taught the Documents.

sentences.zh: 舉孝廉。

translations.literal: He was recommended as filial and incorrupt.
translations.idiomatic: He was recommended on the filial-and-incorrupt list.

sentences.zh: 永平中，以謁者守侍御史，與三府掾屬共考案楚獄顏忠、王平等，辭連及隧鄉侯耿建、朗陵侯臧信、護澤侯鄧鯉、曲成侯劉建。

translations.literal: In the Yongping period, as herald acting attendant censor, together with three offices' clerks and subordinates he jointly examined the Chu prison case of Yan Zhong, Wang Ping, and others; their statements implicated Sui Xiang Marquis Geng Jian, Langling Marquis Zang Xin, Huze Marquis Deng Li, Qucheng Marquis Liu Jian.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yongping he served as herald and acting censor, joining staff from the three high offices to retry the Chu conspiracy case against Yan Zhong and Wang Ping. Their confessions dragged in Marquis Geng Jian of Suixiang, Marquis Zang Xin of Langling, Marquis Deng Li of Huze, and Marquis Liu Jian of Qucheng.

sentences.zh: 建等辭未嘗與忠、平相見。

translations.literal: Jian and others' statements had never met Zhong and Ping.
translations.idiomatic: Geng and the others swore they had never met Yan Zhong or Wang Ping.

sentences.zh: 是時，顯宗怒甚，吏皆惶恐，諸所連及，率一切陷入，無敢以情恕者。

translations.literal: At this time Emperor Ming was greatly angry; clerks all were fearful; all those implicated—generally everything was trapped and sunk; none dared by circumstance show forgiveness.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming was furious; every clerk quaked. Anyone named in the indictments was dragged in wholesale—no one dared plead mercy.

sentences.zh: 朗心傷其冤，試以建等物色獨問忠、平，而二人錯愕不能對。

translations.literal: Lang's heart grieved their injustice; he tried taking Jian and others' appearance alone to question Zhong and Ping, yet the two were flustered and could not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Lang felt the injustice keenly. He separately showed Yan and Wang portraits of Geng Jian and the others they had accused; the pair gaped, unable to recognize them.

sentences.zh: 朗知其詐，乃上言建等無奸，專為忠、平所誣，疑天下無辜類多如此。

translations.literal: Lang knew their deceit; then submitted saying Jian and others had no treachery—purely slandered by Zhong and Ping—he suspected all-under-heaven innocents were mostly thus.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing the fraud, Lang memorialized that Geng Jian and his peers were guiltless and had been framed by Yan and Wang—and that many such wrongful convictions might blanket the empire.

sentences.zh: 帝乃召朗人，問曰：「建等即如是，忠、平何故引之？

translations.literal: The emperor then summoned Lang in and asked, saying: "If Jian and others are thus, why did Zhong and Ping drag them in?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor called Lang in and asked, "If Geng Jian and the rest are innocent, why did Yan Zhong and Wang Ping name them?"

sentences.zh: 」朗對曰：「忠、平自知所犯不道，故多有虛引，冀以自明。

translations.literal: " Lang replied, saying: "Zhong and Ping themselves knew what they committed was not the Way; therefore they had many empty drags; hoping thereby to clarify themselves.
translations.idiomatic: " Lang answered, "They knew their own crimes were capital—and invented accomplices in hope of lightening their guilt."

sentences.zh: 」帝曰：「即如是，四侯無事，何不早奏，獄竟而久係至今邪？

translations.literal: " The emperor said: "If it is thus, the four marquises had no affair—why not early memorialize? The prison ended yet long bound until now?
translations.idiomatic: " The emperor said, "If the four marquises were blameless, why did you not report sooner? Why keep them chained until now?"

sentences.zh: 」郎對曰：「臣雖考之無事，然恐海內別有發其奸者，故未敢時上。

translations.literal: " Lang replied, saying: "Although your servant examined them and found no affair, yet I feared within the seas elsewhere someone might expose their treachery; therefore I did not dare timely submit.
translations.idiomatic: " Lang said, "I found no crime, yet I feared fresh evidence might surface elsewhere—I dared not memorialize until I was certain."

sentences.zh: 」帝怒罵曰：「吏持兩端，促提下。

translations.literal: " The emperor angrily cursed, saying: "Officials hold two ends—hurry drag him down.
translations.idiomatic: " The emperor snarled, "This official sits on the fence—drag him out."

sentences.zh: 」左右方引去，朗曰：「願一言而死。

translations.literal: " Attendants were just leading him away when Lang said: "I wish one word then die.
translations.idiomatic: As guards pulled him away Lang cried, "One sentence before I die!"

sentences.zh: 小臣不敢欺，欲助國耳。

translations.literal: Your petty servant does not dare deceive; I wish to aid the state, that is all.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant does not lie—I meant only to serve the realm."

sentences.zh: 」帝問曰：「誰與共為章？

translations.literal: " The emperor asked, saying: "Who together with you composed the memorial?
translations.idiomatic: " Who helped you draft the memorial?"

sentences.zh: 」對曰：「臣自知當必族滅，不敢多汙染人，誠冀陛下一覺悟而已。

translations.literal: " He replied, saying: "Your servant himself knew I must certainly be clan-extinguished; I did not dare greatly stain others; truly I hoped Your Majesty alone would awaken—that is all.
translations.idiomatic: " Lang said, "I expected my clan to die for this—I would not incriminate others. I only hoped to wake Your Majesty."

sentences.zh: 臣見考囚在事者，咸共言妖惡大故，臣子所宜同疾，今出之不如入之，可無後責。

translations.literal: Your servant sees examining prisoners those in charge—all together say sorcery evil great cause—what ministers and sons ought jointly to hate; now releasing them is not as good as imprisoning them—can avoid later blame.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone handling the case cried sorcery and treason—crimes every loyal subject must abhor. Better jail the innocent than free them and risk blame later.

sentences.zh: 是以考一連十，考十連百。

translations.literal: Therefore examine one connects ten; examine ten connects a hundred.
translations.idiomatic: So one suspect led to ten, and ten to a hundred.

sentences.zh: 又公卿朝會，陛下問以得失，皆長跪言，舊制大罪禍及九族，陛下大恩，裁止於身，天下幸甚。

translations.literal: Moreover dukes and ministers at court assembly—Your Majesty asks about gain and loss—all kneel long and speak: old system great crimes calamity reached nine clans; Your Majesty's great grace cuts only at the person—all-under-heaven greatly fortunate.
translations.idiomatic: At audience you ask our counsel; we kneel and praise your mercy—the old law struck nine kin for one rebel, but you punish only the offender. The realm rejoices.

sentences.zh: 及其歸舍，口雖不言，而仰屋竊歎，莫不知其多冤，無敢牾陛下者。

translations.literal: When they return home, although mouth does not speak, yet looking up at the roof they secretly sigh—none does not know how many are wronged—none dares oppose Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: Yet at home we stare at the rafters and sigh—everyone knows how many innocents rot in jail, and no one dares contradict you.

sentences.zh: 臣今所陳，誠死無悔。

translations.literal: What your servant now presents—truly in death no regret.
translations.idiomatic: What I say now I will stand by to the death.

sentences.zh: 」帝意解，詔遣朗出。

translations.literal: " The emperor's intent dissolved; edict ordered Lang sent out.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor relented and ordered Lang released.

sentences.zh: 後二日，車駕自幸洛陽獄錄囚徒，理出千餘人。

translations.literal: After two days the imperial carriage personally visited Luoyang prison to record prisoners; orderly released more than a thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: Two days later the emperor inspected Luoyang prison himself and freed over a thousand inmates.

sentences.zh: 後平、忠死獄中，朗乃自繫。

translations.literal: Later Ping and Zhong died in prison; Lang then bound himself.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Ping and Yan Zhong died in custody, Lang placed himself under arrest.

sentences.zh: 會赦，免官。

translations.literal: It happened that there was amnesty; he was dismissed from office.
translations.idiomatic: An amnesty cleared him but stripped his post.

sentences.zh: 復舉孝廉。

translations.literal: Again he was recommended filial and incorrupt.
translations.idiomatic: He was recommended again on the filial-and-incorrupt list.

sentences.zh: 永初三年，太尉張禹薦朗為博士，徵詣公車，會卒，時年八十四。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yongchu, Grand Commandant Zhang Yu recommended Lang as erudit; he was summoned to the public carriage office—it happened he died, aged eighty-four.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 3 Grand Commandant Zhang Yu nominated Lang for the imperial academy; the summons reached the public carriage office the day Lang died, aged eighty-four.

sentences.zh: 論曰：左丘明有言：「仁人之言，其利博哉！

translations.literal: The judgment says: Zuo Qiuming had a saying: "The humane man's words—their benefit is broad!
translations.idiomatic: Appreciation: Zuo Qiuming said, "The words of a humane man spread wide benefit!

sentences.zh: 」晏子一言，齊侯省刑。

translations.literal: " One word of Yanzi—the marquis of Qi reduced punishments.
translations.idiomatic: One word from Yan Ying moved the duke of Qi to ease punishments.

sentences.zh: 若鍾離意之就格請過，寒朗之廷爭冤獄，篤矣乎，仁者之情也！

translations.literal: As for Zhongli Yi's approaching the frame requesting fault, Han Lang's court disputing wrongful prisons—deep indeed! This is the humane man's intent!
translations.idiomatic: Think of Zhongli Yi offering his back for the cane and Han Lang pleading innocent lives in court—how deep runs that humane spirit!

sentences.zh: 夫正直本於忠誠則不詭，本於諫爭則絞切。

translations.literal: As for uprightness rooted in loyalty then not deceitful; rooted in remonstrance and dispute then urgent and sharp.
translations.idiomatic: Uprightness rooted in loyalty is never false; rooted in remonstrance it cuts sharp.

sentences.zh: 彼二子之所本得乎天，故言信而志行也。

translations.literal: Those two men's what they rooted obtained from heaven; therefore speech was trustworthy and intent was practiced.
translations.idiomatic: Those two drew their courage from heaven itself—so their words rang true and their resolve held.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：伯魚、子阿，矯急去苛。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Boyu, Zi'e—sternly urgent, removed harshness.
translations.idiomatic: Encomium: Boyu and Zi'e—stern men who swept away cruelty.

sentences.zh: 臨官以潔，匡帝以奢。

translations.literal: Facing office with purity; correcting the emperor with extravagance.
translations.idiomatic: They ruled with purity and checked an extravagant throne.

sentences.zh: 宋均達政，禁此妖禜。

translations.literal: Song Jun comprehended government; forbade these sorcerous sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Song Jun grasped governance and stamped out occult cults.

sentences.zh: 禽蟲畏德，子民請病。

translations.literal: Birds and insects feared virtue; the people begged his illness.
translations.idiomatic: Beasts and insects felt his virtue; the people prayed when he fell ill.

sentences.zh: 意明尊尊，割恩蕃屏。

translations.literal: Yi clarified honoring the honored; cut grace, feudatory screens.
translations.idiomatic: Yi upheld rank and severed favor to send princes to their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 惵惵楚黎，寒君為命。

translations.literal: Trembling Chu black-haired—Lord Han was their life.
translations.idiomatic: The trembling people of Chu owed Han Lang their lives.
